mission.title: backstage: Support Google Analytics 4 as a analytics integration
mission.description: Support Google Analytics 4 as a analytics integration. Requested by 25+ users.

steps.title: Check current backstage setup
steps.description:
Verify your backstage version and configuration:
```bash
npx backstage-cli info
```
This feature requires a working backstage installation.

steps.title: Review backstage configuration
steps.description:
Review the relevant backstage configuration:
Right now only older versions of Google Analytics are supported by the [`/plugin-analytics-module-ga`](https://github.com/backstage/backstage/tree/master/plugins/analytics-module-ga).

steps.title: Apply the fix for Support Google Analytics 4 as a analytics integration
steps.description:
## Hey, I just made a Pull Request!

This plugin provides an opinionated implementation of the Backstage Analytics
API for Google Analytics 4. Once installed and configured, analytics events will
be sent to GA as your users navigate and use your Backstage instance.

This plugin contains no other functionality.

#### :heavy_check_mark: Checklist

- [X ] A changeset describing the change and affected packages. ([more

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected.
Confirm the feature described in "Support Google Analytics 4 as a analytics integration" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
## Hey, I just made a Pull Request!

This plugin provides an opinionated implementation of the Backstage Analytics
API for Google Analytics 4. Once installed and configured, analytics events will
be sent to GA as your users navigate and use your Backstage instance.

prerequisites.description: A working backstage installation or development environment.
